summary: The unit tests (ArtifactCleanup.Tests.ps1) provide decent coverage of core policies (MaxAge, MaxTotalSizeMB, KeepLatestN, dry-run, summary formatting) with 13 test cases and good Pester structure. However, critical gaps exist: (1) The task explicitly requires 'every single test case must execute through the GitHub Actions workflow via act' with 'for each test case: set up a temp git repo... run act push --rm', but Run-Tests.ps1 only executes ONE act invocation, not per-test-case; (2) Insufficient rigor—missing edge cases (empty artifact lists, boundary conditions like artifact created exactly at MaxAgeDays, zero/negative policy values, very large counts, size precision); (3) No error handling or invalid-input validation tested; (4) The workflow file .github/workflows/artifact-cleanup-script.yml is referenced but not provided, preventing full evaluation; (5) No test isolation beyond BeforeAll; (6) The required act-result.txt output file is not demonstrated. The test harness structure is present but does not implement the multi-case orchestration pattern required by the task. Unit test design is reasonable but rigor is weak—happy path only.
